task: I’m putting together a small campaign page that needs to work on both mobile and desktop, and I want to sanity-check the setup before I share it. Can you help me think through what usually needs to be verified so the page, form, and tracking behave as expected across devices?

prefs.explanation: He values clear communication and efficiency, so he likely prefers responses that get to the point quickly. He probably does not want a lot of hedging or filler when he is trying to make a decision or fix something. Directness fits his practical, work-focused style.

His work style suggests he wants information delivered without unnecessary length. He tends to focus on what is actionable, so shorter responses with only the needed context will likely feel most useful. Long explanations may be acceptable only when the problem is genuinely complex.

He seems best served by help that comes in manageable steps rather than a flood of information at once. His real-world problem solving is practical and sequential, so chunked guidance would match how he likely works through tasks. Too many steps upfront could slow him down.

He appears to prefer organized, efficient presentation over free-form prose. Bullets or checklists would fit his need to compare options, track tasks, and avoid missing details. This format also matches his practical approach to work and decisions.

He seems to respond best to steady, non-dramatic communication. A matter-of-fact tone fits his practical optimism and his preference for reliability over emotional flair. He likely wants encouragement, but not at the expense of clarity.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a practical sanity-check list for a campaign page across mobile and desktop.

## 1) Page basics
Verify:
- page loads correctly on both devices
- headline and offer match the approved version
- images render properly
- no broken layout or overlapping elements
- CTA is visible without excessive scrolling

## 2) Form behavior
Check:
- form fields display correctly
- required fields are enforced
- error messages are clear
- submit button works
- confirmation / thank-you state appears after submit
- autofill doesn’t break formatting

## 3) Tracking
Confirm:
- analytics tag is firing
- conversion event is firing on submit
- UTM parameters are preserved
- click tracking works on CTA buttons
- any pixels / scripts load correctly

## 4) Device-specific checks
### Mobile
- text is readable without zooming
- buttons are easy to tap
- form fields don’t get cut off by the keyboard
- page doesn’t shift weirdly when scrolling
- load time is acceptable on cellular

### Desktop
- layout uses the full width properly
- form and CTA are positioned clearly
- no alignment issues in major browsers

## 5) Final content check
- copy matches approved version
- links go to the right destination
- legal / terms text is present if needed
- contact or support info is correct

## Quick “good to share” rule
Don’t share the page until:
- it works on mobile
- it works on desktop
- form submission is confirmed
- tracking is verified

If you want, I can turn this into a **copy-paste QA checklist** with checkboxes.

per_pref.reason: Starts with a direct answer: "Yes — here’s a practical sanity-check list" and then gives the checklist without hedging or filler.
Concise and actionable; it stays focused on what to verify and avoids long explanations.
Well chunked into sections like "1) Page basics," "2) Form behavior," "3) Tracking," and device-specific subsections.
Uses bullets and checklist-style formatting throughout, exactly matching the preference for organized presentation.
Tone is calm and matter-of-fact, e.g. "practical sanity-check list" and "Don’t share the page until..." without emotional flair.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tight 5-minute QA checklist you can actually run.

## Campaign page QA checklist

### 1) Browser check
- [ ] Chrome
- [ ] Safari
- [ ] Edge, if relevant

### 2) Responsive check
- [ ] Mobile layout works
- [ ] Tablet breakpoint works
- [ ] Desktop layout works
- [ ] No overlap / clipping at common widths

### 3) Form edge cases
- [ ] Blank submit shows error
- [ ] Invalid email format is rejected
- [ ] Long input values don’t break layout
- [ ] Autofill works correctly
- [ ] Submit button works once only

### 4) Tracking
- [ ] Event fires on submit
- [ ] Event fires only once
- [ ] UTM / source data is preserved
- [ ] CTA click tracking works
- [ ] Any pixels/scripts load correctly

### 5) Redirect / confirmation
- [ ] Thank-you page loads correctly
- [ ] Redirect preserves UTM/source data
- [ ] Confirmation state is visible
- [ ] No broken back-button behavior

### 6) Speed
- [ ] Page loads fast on mobile data
- [ ] Images don’t slow the page noticeably
- [ ] No obvious lag on form submit

### 7) Final content
- [ ] Copy matches approved version
- [ ] Links go to the right destination
- [ ] Legal / terms text is present if needed

## Go / no-go
**Go** if:
- page works on mobile, tablet, and desktop
- form edge cases pass
- tracking fires once
- redirect/UTM behavior is correct

**No-go** if:
- any device breaks
- the form fails on edge cases
- tracking double-fires
- source data is lost on redirect

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-line Slack checklist** or a **table with pass/fail columns**.

per_pref.reason: Very direct: opens with "Absolutely — here’s a tight 5-minute QA checklist" and then gives the checklist without hedging.
Concise and actionable; it stays focused on needed checks and avoids long explanation.
Mostly chunked into manageable sections ("1) Browser check" through "7) Final content"), though each section still contains multiple bullets.
Uses bullets, checkboxes, and clear sectioning throughout, exactly matching a checklist format.
Calm, matter-of-fact tone throughout, e.g. "Go / no-go" and straightforward checklist items without emotional flair.
